name: Galina
description: Also known as New Gali, it is a well-developed settlement featuring ornate stone buildings, where the craftsmanship of the marble-work clearly reflects the hands of skilled or talented specialists. They possess a wealth of rare stones and excel in creating traps, nets, and garments in general. Their diet primarily consists of cod, salmon, bison meat, and vegetables grown in their gardens, with potatoes being the staple crop. Bisons are utilized for heavy labor and as mounts. Three mountain clans live together here for mutual benefit.
history: Before the Calamity, three nomadic tribes roamed the region until they found the perfect place to settle: the ancient lagoon of Galina, located atop the hills to the east of their current position. What kept them anchored there was the lake itself, teeming with salmon. They founded their small town, naming it after the lake, and the three tribes began combining their respective skills to help the settlement prosper. Following the Calamity, surviving in the colder heights of the hills became untenable, prompting a migration plan: the Brand clan was responsible for crafting strong, buoyant nets to float supplies down the river. The Sigmond clan focused on using bisons to haul heavy objects down to the river and onto the Brands' nets. Meanwhile, the Trygg clan dismantled the town and its fortifications in a way that preserved the structural integrity, allowing for easy transport and reconstruction. Since Lake Gali flowed into a river that emptied into the northern sea, the clans packed their belongings and let them drift downstream. Thanks to their careful planning, they had all their resources near the sea within 10 days. They didn't know if it would work—especially since some structures arrived damaged and a few people went missing—but the reconstruction began, and over time, the village stood fortified and ready. Twice a year, residents still gather to watch the salmon migrate upstream to spawn and downstream in search of food, an event highly anticipated by everyone.
points_of_interest: Brand Clan House: Mostly composed of simians, this clan house features luxurious beds and refined fabrics scattered throughout. They sell gear to set and disarm traps, as well as high-quality nets, ropes, and clothing.

Sigmond Clan House: Inhabited mostly by halfling druids and hunters, they live alongside bisons to handle heavy labor and hunt monsters to protect the settlement. Specialized in fishing and herbalism, it is said that their stews are the best around.

Trygg Clan House: Powerful smiths and forge-masters, predominantly dwarves, who know exactly how to design cities that command attention through architecture and sheer strength. The settlement's walls are reinforced with their high-quality stone. They love mining, and inside their house, you can find precious gems imbued with magic, as well as weapons, shields, and armor designed to host these stones. They are notorious for being terrible negotiators, undervaluing everything brought to them while charging exorbitant prices for their own crafts.
'The Mug and Cup' Tavern and Inn: Well-known for its drinking competitions.
credits: Thank you Eric for helping me create Galina! <3
